zotero.abstractNote: Non-linear dynamical systems are ubiquitous in science and engineering, yet identifying their latent state transitions remains a significant challenge. We propose a novel framework for identifying latent state transitions in non-linear dynamical systems by leveraging the Koopman operator theory. Our approach learns a global linear representation of the non-linear dynamics in a latent space, where state transitions are identified as changes in the Koopman operator. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on several synthetic and real-world datasets, showing that it outperforms existing methods in identifying latent state transitions and predicting future states.

notes.note: Publication Title: arXiv (Cornell University) Publisher: University of Tübingen
